brief: (downstream: premise pulled from the accepted idea automatically)

gen_answers.theme_statement: This story shows that you should appreciate your rivals, because they make you better.
gen_answers.improved_theme_statement: This story shows that you should appreciate your rivals, because they make you better.
gen_answers.central_question: Is a rival a threat or a gift?
gen_answers.improved_central_question: Is a rival a threat or a gift?
gen_answers.belief_shift: Start: he must beat Ronaldo to be the greatest. End: he appreciates Ronaldo's push.
gen_answers.improved_belief_shift: Start: he must beat Ronaldo to be the greatest. End: he appreciates Ronaldo's push.

gen_answers.theme_statement: This story shows that greatness is forged with your rival, not won against them.
gen_answers.improved_theme_statement: This story shows that greatness is forged with your rival, not won against them.
gen_answers.central_question: Can the man you spent your life trying to beat be the reason you became great?
gen_answers.improved_central_question: Can the man you spent your life trying to beat be the reason you became great?
gen_answers.belief_shift: Start: 'I must defeat him to matter.' End: 'He made me - the rivalry built us both.'
gen_answers.improved_belief_shift: Start: 'I must defeat him to matter.' End: 'He made me - the rivalry built us both.'

gen_answers.theme_statement: This story shows that the rival you try to defeat is the one who makes you whole.
gen_answers.improved_theme_statement: This story shows that the rival you try to defeat is the one who makes you whole.
gen_answers.central_question: Is the rival who haunts you your enemy, or the maker of who you became?
gen_answers.improved_central_question: Is the rival who haunts you your enemy, or the maker of who you became?
gen_answers.belief_shift: Start: greatness is a war I must win alone, against him. End: greatness was shared - he is the reason I became great; there was never a war to win.
gen_answers.improved_belief_shift: Start: greatness is a war I must win alone, against him. End: greatness was shared - he is the reason I became great; there was never a war to win.

gen_answers.theme_statement: This story shows that greatness is never won alone or against someone - it is forged with the rival you spend your life trying to beat.
gen_answers.improved_theme_statement: This story shows that greatness is never won alone or against someone - it is forged with the rival you spend your life trying to beat.
gen_answers.central_question: Is the rival you measure yourself against your enemy, or the only person who made you who you are?
gen_answers.improved_central_question: Is the rival you measure yourself against your enemy, or the only person who made you who you are?
gen_answers.belief_shift: Start: 'To matter, I have to defeat him.' End: 'He is the reason I became great; the rivalry made us both - it was never a war to win.'
gen_answers.improved_belief_shift: Start: 'To matter, I have to defeat him.' End: 'He is the reason I became great; the rivalry made us both - it was never a war to win.'

eval_answers.why: A stance is forming but it reads as a moral ('you should appreciate'), the question is near-binary, and the belief-shift is loose.
Dropped the moral and found the stance; sharpen the tension and make the belief-shift concrete.
Strong stance and a genuine non-yes/no question; name the old and new belief exactly.
Stance, tension, and a concrete belief-shift that hands the structure its misbelief->Recovery - advance.
Confirmed: a theme, not a topic; in real tension; with a belief-shift the outline can dramatize.
